title: US attorney going after money forfeited by Mass. resident in romance crypto fraud scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Federal prosecutors aim to recover $327,000 in cryptocurrency from a romance investment scam targeting a Massachusetts resident.
The scam began in 2024 when "Linda Brown" persuaded a victim on a dating app to invest in a fake crypto opportunity.
Prosecutors filed a civil complaint to seize 327,829.720952 USDT in Tether, claiming the funds were involved in money laundering.

title: Jayson Tatum’s fashion also makes a return in his comeback from Achilles tear
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum made a notable fashion statement with Parisian luxury and '90s nostalgia before his comeback game on Friday.
Tatum achieved a double-double in 27 minutes during his return to the Boston Celtics lineup after an Achilles injury.
His fashion choice and performance were widely shared and discussed, likening his style to Caroline Bessette Kennedy.

title: She survived a brain tumor. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Tracy Haisley, 50, from Columbia, South Carolina, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after surviving a brain tumor.
Haisley underwent brain surgery and three months of rehab, where initially walking was a challenge, illustrating her recovery journey.
She is running with the Boston Public Library team to promote libraries' role in literacy and community connection.

title: What Jaylen Brown said of Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists against the Mavericks.
Jaylen Brown commended Tatum for returning from his Achilles injury, emphasizing their shared goal of winning in Boston.
The Celtics aim for a playoff run, with Brown stressing the importance of overcoming challenges with Tatum back in the lineup.

title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maine native Cooper Flagg expressed his desire to remain with the Mavericks, despite growing up a Celtics fan.
Flagg, the No. 1 draft pick, enjoyed playing in Boston but emphasized his commitment to the Mavericks.
Under contract through 2029, Flagg is averaging 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds in his rookie season.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after a ten-month absence, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists against the Mavericks.
Tatum struggled initially but settled in to help secure a Celtics victory, while the team sits second in the Eastern Conference.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla appreciated Tatum's effort, likening his return to acquiring a player at the trade deadline.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after a 10-month injury hiatus, starting without a minutes restriction and contributing significantly.
Tatum scored 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in the Celtics' 120-100 win over the Mavericks at TD Garden.
Nikola Vucevic is out for a month with a fractured ring finger, undergoing surgery, adding to Celtics' injury concerns.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney chose not to trade key assets at the NHL trade deadline, focusing on long-term growth.
Boston traded Brett Harrison, Jackson Edward, and a draft pick for AHL players Lukas Riechel, Alexis Gendron, and Massimo Rizzo.
The Bruins maintain four first-round picks for future drafts as they rebuild, targeting potential high-impact prospects.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots reportedly lost out on acquiring Maxx Crosby as the Raiders plan to trade him to the Ravens.
Baltimore Ravens will trade two first-round picks to the Raiders for Crosby, including their No. 14 pick.
Crosby expressed interest in joining the Patriots, but New England couldn't match Baltimore's trade compensation.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic fractured his right ring finger and will likely be out for a month.
Vucevic sustained the injury during a game against the Dallas Mavericks after playing only two minutes.
Vucevic has averaged 11.4 points and 8.8 rebounds since joining the Celtics at the trade deadline.
